title: tengine.contract.violation v1
description: One violation emitted per new entry in the contract_violations ring (RES_CONTRACT_VIOLATIONS = 410, WT 590). Highest-volume tengine channel — burst-safe, the ring has 1024 slots so a single frame can emit many events during a violation storm, but only newly-appeared entries are emitted. Producer: crates/tengine-dgc-hal/src/silo/contract_event_emitter.rs (per-frame emitter on the frame-JSONL stream), registry handle CONTRACT_VIOLATION in crates/tengine-dgc-hal/src/silo/shadergen_handlers/track_group.rs. CloudEvents source is 'silo:<silo_name>:<session_id>'; subject is 'frame_<NNNN>'.

frame.description: GPU frame index at which the violation was detected.

race_phase.description: Race-phase enum at the time of detection.

contract_id.description: FNV hex hash identifying the contract, e.g. '0xNNNNNNNN'.

contract_name.description: Human-readable contract name from the ring entry. Empty string for GPU-generated entries that carry no name.

category.description: Contract category — matches the keys used in tengine.contract.state.v1's violations_by_category.

observed_json.description: Array of 4 f32 values as observed by the contract.

expected_json.description: Array of 4 f32 values that were expected.

location_hint.description: Opaque hint (e.g. pod index, voxel coord, or 0 when not applicable).

waypoint_id.description: Cinema (tengine-qc5wv) timeline waypoint id. Always null until Cinema's waypoint wiring lands; the field MUST remain in the schema so existing consumers don't break when Cinema ships.
